title: The CLS Token
concept: The CLS (classification) token is a learnable vector prepended to the patch sequence that aggregates global image information for classification.

exercises.prompt: Recap: What is the sequence of blocks inside a single ViT Encoder block?
exercises.explain: Correct! Layer normalization precedes self-attention and MLP blocks, wrapped in residual connections.

cards.title: The spokesperson
cards.body: If you have a hundred patch tokens, each representing a tiny square of the image, which one gets to speak for the entire image at the classification head? Instead of choosing one patch, we invite a special 'spokesperson' token to join the sequence.

cards.title: The CLS Token
cards.body: The CLS (classification) token is a learnable vector added to the beginning of the patch sequence before entering the Transformer. As it passes through the encoder blocks, self-attention allows it to aggregate representation information from all other patches.

cards.title: The class representative
cards.body: Imagine a room of specialists (patch tokens) discussing an image. We insert a blank notepad (the CLS token) at the start. During the discussion (self-attention), every specialist writes summary notes onto the notepad. At the end, we read only this notepad to make our final decision.

cards.title: From sequence to class
cards.body: If an image is divided into 196 patches, we prepend 1 CLS token, making the sequence length 197. After 12 encoder blocks, we discard the 196 patch outputs and feed only the single 1-dimensional CLS token output into the classifier.

cards.title: Not the only way
cards.body: You might think a CLS token is mandatory for ViT. While popular, alternative methods like **Global Average Pooling** (averaging all final patch token vectors) work just as well and don't require an extra token.

left.text:
1 extra learnable token prepended
Aggregates info via attention
Classifier reads only this token

right.text:
No extra tokens prepended
Averages final patch vectors
Classifier reads mean representation

exercises.prompt: Why is the CLS token prepended to the sequence of patch embeddings?
exercises.explain: The CLS token's job is to gather global representations from all patches via self-attention for the final classification task.

options.text: To serve as a global representation aggregator that the classifier can read
To double the spatial resolution of the input image
To apply convolutional filters to the inputs
To store the coordinates of each patch

exercises.prompt: True or false: The values inside the CLS token are fixed and never change during training.
exercises.explain: False. The CLS token starts as a learnable parameter that is optimized during training along with the rest of the model weights.
Global Average Pooling averages the features of all patches to form the final image representation.
exercises.sentence: Instead of using a CLS token, some models use Global Average ___ across all final patch embeddings.

exercises.prompt: If a model splits an image into 256 patches and prepends a CLS token, what is the sequence length input to the Transformer?
exercises.explain: Adding the 1 CLS token to the 256 image patches yields a sequence of 257 tokens.

exercises.prompt: How does self-attention enable the CLS token to represent the entire image?

options.text: Because the CLS token computes attention weights with respect to all patch tokens, letting it pull in features from every location.
Because the CLS token is forced to copy the middle patch of the image.
Because it applies a pooling operation over the raw image pixels directly.
Because the CLS token acts as a high-pass spatial frequency filter.

exercises.explain: Brilliant! Through self-attention layers, the CLS token computes relationships with every other patch in the sequence, gradually accumulating global representations. Next: how the model knows which patch is where!
